back_translation: Paul, who is a slave of Jesus Christ, who has been (royally) called to be an apostle, who has been set apart for the good news of God
which God (royally) promised in advance through His prophets in the holy Scriptures
concerning His Son, who (royally) was born from the lineage of David according to the flesh
who (royally) was appointed to be the Son of God with power, according to the Spirit of holiness, by the (royal) resurrection from death — namely Jesus Christ, our Lord
Through Him we received grace and the apostleship, in order to bring about the obedience that comes from faith among all the nations, for the sake of His name
Among those nations, you also are — being those who have been (royally) called to belong to Jesus Christ
To everyone in Rome who is beloved of God, who has been (royally) called to be saints: may grace and peace from God our Father, and from Jesus Christ our Lord, abide with all of you
First of all, I give thanks to my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith has been proclaimed throughout the world
For God (royally) is my witness — He whom I serve with my spirit in the good news of His Son — that I remember you unceasingly
always in my prayers, requesting that now at last I may succeed in coming to you — according to the will of God
For I long to meet you, so that I may share some spiritual gift with you, in order that you may be strengthened and made firm
That is, that we may receive mutual encouragement together among you, through the faith we have for one another — both yours and mine

Brothers, I do not want you to be unaware that I have intended many times to come to you, but have been hindered up to now, so that I might have some fruit among you also, just as among the other nations
I am one who is in debt — both to Greeks and to barbarians, both to those who have wisdom and to those who lack understanding
Therefore, on my part I am eager to proclaim the good news to you who are in Rome also
For I am not ashamed of the good news, because that good news is the power of God leading to salvation, for everyone who believes — to the Jew first, and also to the Greek
For in that good news, the righteousness of God is revealed, by faith from the beginning to the end, just as it is written: 'The righteous one will live by faith'
For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all the godlessness and unrighteousness of humanity, those who suppress the truth by means of unrighteousness
Because what can be known about God is plainly seen among them, for God (royally) has revealed it to them already

For from the creation of the world, His invisible attributes — namely His eternal power and His being-God-ness — are clearly seen, being understood through the things that He (royally) has created; therefore humanity has no excuse
Because although they knew God, they did not give glory to Him as is fitting for God, nor give thanks; but they became empty in their thinking, and their foolish heart-minds were darkened
While claiming to be wise ones, they instead became fools
And exchanged the glory of God who (royally) is imperishable for replicas resembling perishable man, and birds, four-footed animals, and creeping things
Therefore God (royally) released them according to the lusts in their hearts, into uncleanness, so that their bodies were dishonored among one another

They are the ones who exchanged the truth of God for falsehood, and worshiped and served the thing that was (royally) created instead of worshiping and serving the (royal) Creator, who (royally) is praised forever and ever — Amen
For this reason, God (royally) released them into shameful passions of lust; for even their women exchanged natural relations for what is contrary to nature

Likewise the men also abandoned natural relations with women, burning as fire with lust toward one another — men with men committing what is shameful, and receiving the fitting penalty for their error within themselves
And since they did not see fit to have God in their knowledge, God (royally) released them into a corrupted mind, to do what is not fitting
Filled with all unrighteousness, depravity, greed, cruel-heartedness; overflowing with envy, killing, quarreling, deceit, malicious-thinking; being whisper-gossipers
Slanderers, haters of God, the insolent, the haughty, the boastful, inventors of evil things, those who do not obey their parents
Lacking understanding, breakers of agreements, those without natural affection, those without mercy
They, although they know the righteous decree of God — that those who do such things deserve death — not only do those things, but also approve of others who do them
